description_long: This object is a classic, early-generation mobile phone with a rectangular, boxy form factor and a prominent, long black antenna extending from the top. The front face features a small speaker grille at the top, a numeric keypad with raised buttons, and a single orange button below the keypad, all set within a recessed panel. The body is primarily a light gray plastic with subtle seams and a slightly textured finish, while the antenna appears to be made of a darker, matte plastic or rubber. The sides and back are plain, with no visible ports or additional features, emphasizing the utilitarian design typical of mobile phones from the 1980s and early 1990s. The overall construction suggests a solid, durable build, with the keypad and speaker as the main interactive elements. This type of device would have been commonly seen in urban environments during the early days of mobile communication.
description: A vintage, boxy mobile phone with a long black antenna, light gray plastic body, and a front numeric keypad with an orange button.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, full numeric keypad, and orange button below the keypad.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the plain, flat surface and the protruding antenna at the top.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a featureless, smooth back with the antenna extending upward.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with a plain surface and visible antenna.
attribute_screen_display_content: No visible screen or display content; the area above the keypad is a speaker grille.
attribute_camera_placement: No visible camera modules or lenses on any side of the object.
attribute_button_layout: Twelve raised numeric buttons and one orange button arranged in a grid on the front below the speaker grille.
